steps.title: Set up the quadratic solution form
steps.content:
The answer choices are written as exact radical expressions for the solutions, so solve the quadratic and then match the simplified form. For $x^2 - 2x - 9 = 0$, the coefficients are $a = 1$, $b = -2$, and $c = -9$. First compute the discriminant:
$$b^2 - 4ac = (-2)^2 - 4(1)(-9) = 4 + 36 = 40$$
Then substitute into the quadratic formula:
$$x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} = \frac{2 \pm \sqrt{40}}{2}$$

steps.title: Simplify the radical and extract k
steps.content:
Simplify $\sqrt{40} = \sqrt{4 \cdot 10} = 2\sqrt{10}$. Substitute back to get:
$$x = \frac{2 \pm 2\sqrt{10}}{2} = \frac{2(1 \pm \sqrt{10})}{2} = 1 \pm \sqrt{10}$$
One solution is $x = 1 + \sqrt{10}$. Comparing to the form $1 + \sqrt{k}$, we see that $k = 10$. That is the correct answer.
